A.example_answer: Reaction center atoms: enolate carbon [CH2:10] attacks the α,β-unsaturated system [CH:20]=[CH:21]‑[C:22](=O) with the carbonyl oxygen [O:23]; the new C–C bond between [CH2:10] and [CH:20] closes the ring and the carbonyl becomes the enolate [O-:23] with Li+ as counterion.

B.example_answer: Immediate precursors: 3,4-dichlorophenylhydrazine and a methoxy-substituted β-ketoester such as ethyl 2-methoxyacetoacetate.

C.example_answer: 1. Cyclize 4-bromo-2-cyanobenzaldehyde with hydrazine hydrate under acidic conditions to form 6-bromo-3-cyanoindazole. 2. Convert 5-bromo-2-methylbenzoic acid to the acid chloride with SOCl2 and amidate with cyclopropylamine to afford 5-bromo-2-methyl-N-cyclopropylbenzamide. 3. Borylate the aryl bromide via Miyaura borylation (B2pin2, Pd(dppf)Cl2, KOAc) to generate the boronic ester. 4. Suzuki couple this boronate with 6-bromo-3-cyanoindazole (Pd(PPh3)4/K2CO3, dioxane/H2O) to yield the target molecule, then purify.

run_note: A and B completed with 10 records each; C was intentionally stopped after 2 records because per-item latency was too high for a smoke run.
